overview: The TXML Filter processes **Wordfast Pro TXML documents**, a proprietary XML-based bilingual format used by Wordfast Pro and supported by several other tools. It extracts source and target content from segmented bilingual entries, preserving existing translations and handling revisions. There are no official public specifications for the TXML format.

output.allowEmptyOutputTarget.help:
Controls what happens when a segment has no translation at merge time.

- **Enabled**: empty target segments are written as-is (empty) in the output.
- **Disabled**: the source text is copied into the target when no translation exists, ensuring every segment has target content.

The pre-configured variant **"TXML (Fill empty targets in output)"** has this option disabled.
notes: The `okf_txml-fillEmptyTargets` configuration sets this to `false`, copying source text into empty targets on output.

processingNotes: **Encoding detection**: Uses the XML encoding declaration if present; otherwise defaults to UTF-8 regardless of any externally specified default encoding.
**BOM handling**: A UTF-8 BOM is preserved in output only if the input was also UTF-8 and contained a BOM. Transcoding from non-UTF-8 to UTF-8 never adds a BOM.
**XML encoding declaration**: Automatically updated on output if present, or added if missing.
**Line breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break style as the input document.
**Segmentation**: TXML blocks contain one or more segments. Each block is extracted as a single text unit with all its segments.
**Existing translations**: Target text is extracted alongside source. Segments marked `gtmt="true"` without `modified="true"` are also stored as `AltTranslationsAnnotation`.
**Revisions**: Only the latest translation is extracted; `<revisions>` elements are ignored. Merging overwrites the previous translation without creating revision entries.
limitations: The TXML format does not allow different source and target content for inter-segment parts. If these parts change after extraction, the difference cannot be represented at merge time — only the source representation is preserved.
No official public specification exists for the TXML format.
